name: python3-git-delete-merged-branches
title: python3-git-delete-merged-branches
summary: command-line tool to delete merged git branches (Python module)
description:
command-line tool to delete merged git branches (Python module)
A convenient command-line tool helping you keep repositories clean.
.
Features:
* Supports deletion of both local and remote branches
* Detects multiple forms of de-facto merges (rebase merges, squash merges
(needs --effort=3), single or range cherry-picks… leveraging git cherry)
* Supports workflows with multiple release branches, e.g. only delete
branches that have been merged to all of master, dev and staging
* Quick interactive configuration
* Provider agnostic: Works with GitHub, GitLab, Gitea and any other Git
hosting
* Takes safety seriously
.
This package contains the Python module.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use python3-git-delete-merged-branches because command-line tool to delete merged git branches (Python module).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use python3-git-delete-merged-branches as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair python3-git-delete-merged-branches with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `python3-git-delete-merged-branches --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
